untranslatable_reason: The signal requires calculating fair value premium (Index Value × [1 + Time in Years × (Risk-free Rate - Dividend Yield)]) and comparing futures price to this dynamically computed value. This requires real-time calculation of fair value using external inputs (risk-free rate, dividend yield, time to expiration, index level) that are not available as standard indicators in the allowed indicator list. The premium threshold itself (3.50-4.00 points) is static, but the fair value benchmark is formula-driven and cannot be expressed using only the provided indicators.
translation_notes: Signal is an arbitrage strategy requiring fair value calculation. Used close price as proxy with static threshold of 3.75 points (midpoint of 3.50-4.00 range) as a placeholder, but this does not capture the intended fair value comparison logic. A proper implementation would require custom indicator for fair value premium calculation or external data feeds for risk-free rate and dividend yield inputs.
